'''Darren Brown''' (born 29 April 1969) is an [[Australians|Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]].  Brown also played for [[Chorley Lynx|Trafford Borough]] in [[England]] and for the [[Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent most of his career as a lock or second rower, but could also play in the backline.

==Background==
Brown was born in [[Sydney]] and raised in [[Redfern, New South Wales]]. He played his junior football with [[Zetland Magpies]], [[Mascot Jets]] and Waterloo Waratahs in the [[South Sydney District Junior Rugby Football League|South Sydney Juniors]], as well as the Brighton Seagulls in the St George Juniors.{{citation needed|date=April 2022}}

==Rugby league career==
Brown began his first-grade career at South Sydney (first grade player #774)<ref name="auto1">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_b/player_brown_darren.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report - Darren Brown|website=www.ssralmanac.com}}</ref> in the [[1989 NSWRL season]] and had two stints at the club.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/darren-brown/summary.html|title = Darren Brown - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article122408256 |title=Brown serves second dose of revenge |newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=6 April 1992 |accessdate=28 June 2019 |page=19 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref><ref>{{cite AV media| url-status = live| archive-url = https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211210/hBLnQOgDPK8| archive-date = 2021-12-10| url = https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hBLnQOgDPK8&t=98s| title = SOUTH SYDNEY RABBITOHS 1989 | website=[[YouTube]]}}{{cbignore}}</ref>

In between his South Sydney stints he played the [[1990 NSWRL season|1990]] off season with [[English people|English]] club [[Chorley Lynx|Trafford Borough]]<ref>{{cite AV media| url-status = live| archive-url = https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211210/G0RvdxyNGYc| archive-date = 2021-12-10| url = https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G0RvdxyNGYc| title = Trafford Borough V Nottingham City 1990 | website=[[YouTube]]}}{{cbignore}}</ref> and then returned to Australia for the [[1991 NSWRL season|1991 season]] with Canterbury (first grade player #559),<ref>{{cite web|url=https://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=559 |title=Darren Brown |website=thebulldogs.com.au |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140411012411/https://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=559 |archive-date=11 April 2014}}</ref> where he featured as a centre and [[five-eighth]], with a highlight being a [[hat-trick]] of tries in a win over [[Sydney Roosters|Eastern Suburbs]].<ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article122351590 |title=Taylor blunders damage Magpies |newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=25 March 1991 |accessdate=28 June 2019 |page=24 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref>

After returning to South Sydney for a further two years,<ref name="auto1"/> Brown joined Western Suburbs (first grade player #974)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/|title=First Grade Players - Western Suburbs Magpies|access-date=7 May 2020|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090901/http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref> in the [[1994 NSWRL season|1994 season]] and played lock in the first three rounds. He was then sacked by Western Suburbs following his involvement in a brawl outside Canterbury Leagues Club and finished the season with Penrith (first grade player #361).<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://themightypanthers.wordpress.com/2018/08/20/players-darren-brown/|title=PLAYERS | Darren Brown|date=20 August 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article118116503 |title=Brown, Penrith linked |newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=30 April 1994 |accessdate=28 June 2019 |page=60 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref>

Used as a forward, Brown continued to play for Penrith for another four seasons, which included two finals appearances<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-1997/minor-prelim-semi/canterbury-vs-penrith/summary.html|title = Super League 1997 - Minor Prelim Semi - Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-1997/minor-semi/canberra-vs-penrith/summary.html|title = Super League 1997 - Minor Semi - Rugby League Project}}</ref> in the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season|1997 Super League season]].<ref name="auto"/>

From 1999 to 2001 he returned to play in England for the [[Salford Red Devils]] (first grade player #965),<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/club/salford-red-devils-heritage-list/ |title=Heritage List |website=Salford Red Devils |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191027081918/https://www.salfordreddevils.net/club/salford-red-devils-heritage-list/ |archive-date=27 October 2019}}</ref> which he captained.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Fisher |first1=Michael |title=Salford boosted by Brown return |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2995829/Salford-boosted-by-Brown-return.html |work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]] |date=4 January 2001}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Since retiring from rugby league Brown has worked as a [[real estate broker|real estate agent]]<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/southern-courier/maroubra-mates-walk-gold-coast-to-sydney-in-name-of-mental-health/news-story/a97038eb23443e96a2921773eae83f1c|title = From rough trot to an epic journey|newspaper = Daily Telegraph|date = 11 October 2016|last1 = Seiler|first1 = Melissa}}</ref> and is currently employed as a [[stevedore]] (wharfie) on the [[Port Botany (seaport)|Port Botany]] waterfront. In 2016 Brown walked 858 [[kilometre]]s from the [[Gold Coast, Queensland|Gold Coast]] to Sydney with his mates from Rise Foundation Australia to raise awareness for [[suicide prevention]] and [[mental health]] issues.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/manly-daily/sport/struggling-league-players-urged-to-seek-help-as-two-stars-take-their-own-lives/news-story/4275db868d0466a56bdfa5d1c92076a8|title = Plea to struggling league players|newspaper = Daily Telegraph|date = 9 January 2017|last1 = Geddes|first1 = Jon}}</ref> In 2019 Brown began co-hosting ''Rabbitohs Radio'', a weekly rugby league [[podcast]] covering the South Sydney Rabbitohs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://rabbitohsradio.com.au/2019/10/29/the-rabbitohs-radio-story/|title=Season 1 The Rabbitohs Radio Story |last=Mavin |first=Steve |website=Rabbitohs Radio |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200928230400/https://rabbitohsradio.com.au/2019/10/29/the-rabbitohs-radio-story/ |archive-date=28 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://podcasts.apple.com/au/podcast/rabbitohs-radio/id1473739413|title=RABBITOHS RADIO on Apple Podcasts|date=19 August 2023 }}</ref> In the podcast Brown and close mate Grant Chappell examine Rabbitohs team [[news]], performances and [[interview]] special guests.<ref>{{cite AV media| url-status = live| archive-url = https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211210/3DoC2eLO9GI| archive-date = 2021-12-10| url = https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3DoC2eLO9GI&t=316s| title = Rabbitohs Radio In The Studio | website=[[YouTube]]}}{{cbignore}}</ref>
